message:
fix(async): prevent waker thread proliferation in OneshotFuture polling

The OneshotFuture poll implementation was spawning a new waker thread on
every Pending poll, causing thread leak under work-stealing schedulers
that re-poll frequently. Now spawns exactly one waker thread on the first
Empty state and stores a shared Arc<Mutex<Option<Waker>>> so subsequent
polls just update the waker atomically (handles executor waker rotation).

Also includes beads issue tracker metadata update.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
